Thanks for contributing! A few quick asks:

- Title your PR with a Conventional Commits prefix:
  feat: | fix: | refactor: | docs: | test: | chore: | ci:
- Cite the spec section your change relates to (or propose an
  amendment under "Spec impact" below).
- Keep PRs reviewable — smaller, focused diffs land faster.
-->

<!-- What does this PR do, in 1–3 sentences? Focus on the *why*. -->

## Spec impact

<!--
- If this is a pure bug fix, cite the section(s) whose contract was
  being violated and will now hold (e.g. "§7.3 idempotency fingerprint").
- If this is an amendment, describe the current contract, the new
  contract, and the migration story for consumers.
- If this is docs/tooling only, say "none".
-->

## Changes

<!-- Bullet list of the notable changes. Keep it skimmable. -->

## Tests

Every behavioural change needs a test. Describe:
- what new/changed test(s) cover the behaviour
- the scenarios they exercise
If the change is untestable, say so and why.
-->

## Checklist

- [ ] I ran `make test` locally and it passed.
- [ ] I ran `make lint` (or CI lint job) and it passed.
- [ ] I updated `CHANGELOG.md` under `[Unreleased]`.
- [ ] I updated docs / README / spec where behaviour changed.
- [ ] This PR does not introduce secrets, credentials, or real data.
- [ ] I linked any related issues below.
